entry.title: Re: SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition Subscription failed
entry.content:
Hello, 

I have found a way to make it happen. 
Click here and follow the steps
👇🏼👇🏼👇🏼
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-build-work-zone-standard-edition/ba-p/14087118

entry.title: Re: Calling OData services in SAP Digital Mnaufacturing
entry.content:
Hi Harsh-ctrl, i know that has been a while but...

 

1) i dont know if you are using Cloud Connector or not, but i'm assuming YES

2) you should use only http://{host}:{port}  on VIRTUAL HOST on servers

3) on your custom service registry you should attach your WebServer (as you did) but on the url/path you should not attach the entire url, but just the path that arrives by removing the web servers path, like this:

Path WebServer host/virtual url:  https://xxxx.yyyyy.com:443  (the same configured inside Cloud Connector)

 
Path Service Registry: /sap/opu/odata/SAP/XXXXXXXXXXXX/A_bbbbbbb

entry.title: Re: Calling OData services in SAP Digital Mnaufacturing
entry.content:
@cicero1988 for your issue:

- check user and password on SAP BTP destination, check that they are working fine
- second check: check inside your production process that the destination has been selected!

Let me know

entry.title: Re: Calling OData services in SAP Digital Mnaufacturing
entry.content: to be removed

entry.title: Re: Physical path for logical file
entry.content:
Hi,

Create the directories in such a way that it is under a single common directory, the common directory can be maintained in SFILE so that all the directories under the common directory can be used for uploading via CG3Z.

Best Regards,

Anirudh P S

entry.title: Re: P&G - New Step in the Route Map when rejecting the form
entry.content: Hello there is no way of auto route step creation based on reject button

entry.title: Re: S/4 HANA Extensibility – Guide to extend API_BUSINESS_PARTNER API for Standard Fields
entry.content: Would it work for update as well or is it only for reading data?

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud Variable Prompt
entry.content:
Hi,

What you are seeing is the standard behaviour for ‘F4’ help in BEx queries. This is governed by the backend in BW. In order to have ‘cascading’ variables you would need to implement the BADI RSR_VARIABLE_F4_RESTRICT in BW. See https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/bex-cascading-variable/qaq-p/11895992 for example. 
Cheers, Ivan.

entry.title: Re: Extract configuration from Integration Design artifacts via API
entry.content:
Hi Javier,

can you pls share where/what you needed to change, to have it working in Cloud Foundry. there is error - probably you had the same...

BR

Mateusz

entry.title: Re: SAP Workzone Content Provider from another subaccount error - Design Time Destination
entry.content: Hello, To help me understand the issue better, could you please provide the following details of your destination configuration: URL: Please provide the exact URL you're using. Authentication Method: What authentication method are you using (e.g., NoAuthentication, BasicAuthentication, OAuth2ClientCredentials)? Additional Properties: Are there any additional properties configured for the destination? If so, please provide their names and values. Providing these details will help me analyze the configuration and identify potential issues.

entry.title: Hi, I am facing the same issue.Can you share how you solved it?
SAP Hana not starting after upgrade (localsecurestore not starting up)
entry.content:
Hi,

After an upgrade of our HANA from 2.00.081 to 2.00.083 the HANA does not start up correctly. When running HDB start I can clearly see that all components are starting up and are working fine except localsecurestore. I not able to access the database using HANA studio, the startup process never finishes. The main error I encounter at the daemon log file are:

Daemon.cpp(00756) : Found terminated and planned instance 0 of program 'localsecurestore'

This line is always popping up every 30 seconds in the logs as seen above.

I have found another post with this issue, but no solution was posted.

Anyone has an idea of how to solve this?

I have opened a case with sap but have not got an answer yet.

Any help would really be appreciated.

Regards,

entry.title: SAP Datasphere and SAP HANA Cloud — Technical Activities During Table/View Creation, Persistence
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I would appreciate your guidance on the following questions related to SAP Datasphere and SAP HANA Cloud:

When we create a table in SAP Datasphere:

  • What operations are performed on the underlying SAP HANA Cloud database?

  • Is a physical table immediately created in HANA Cloud?

  • Are there any additional metadata artifacts generated at this stage?

When we create a view in SAP Datasphere:

  • How is this view represented on the HANA Cloud database side (e.g., SQL view, calculation view)?

  • Is any temporary or runtime structure created immediately?

When we persist data:

  • Into a table:
    What activities occur in HANA Cloud during data persistence into an existing Datasphere table?

  • Into a view (after enabling persistence):
    How is the persisted data stored in the underlying database?
    Is a new table automatically created? If yes, what is its structure relative to the original view definition?

 Regarding Partitioning and Data Loading:

  • When partitions are created on a Datasphere table or persisted view:

    • What database-level changes happen on SAP HANA Cloud?

  • When we load data into these partitions:

    • Are partitions managed internally as standard HANA partitions, or handled separately?

  • When we delete specific partitions or delete all partitions:

    • What database activities happen during this operation?

General Runtime Activities:

  • During all these operations (table/view creation, persistence, partitioning, deletion), what are the typical HANA Cloud runtime actions (e.g., commits, locks, internal optimizations, delta merges)?

Any insights, technical explanations, or references to SAP Datasphere documentation would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance for your support!

entry.title: BTP Trial Account: Cloud Connector disconnected
entry.content:
I am using a BTP Trial Account to prepare for the Low Code/No Code Certification.

I was previously (a few weeks ago) able to create Processes in my Trial Account without any issues.
Now, when trying to create a new Process or edit one which was already created, I am faced with this constant error popup :

"Request failed with status code 400
Unable to recognize the artifact type [notificationtemplate]"

I haven't edited the default 'notificationtemplate' - I don't even know where to check that or how we would edit it. Upon checking SAP For Me I could not find anything related to artifact type errors in BTP / SAP Build. The closest I could find were issues relating to the Cloud Connector.

So, I checked in my Trial Account > Connectivity > Cloud Connectors and I can see:

There is no cloud connector connected to this subaccount.


Request Details
statusCode:404
reason:Not Found
request:/api/v1/subaccount/497134c1-e390-4384-b763-9e02eee8159e/info
timestamp:26.04.2025 12:31:33
response:<empty>

I hadn't previously configured a Cloud Connector for this account and I don't know if it's required, but I was previously able to create Process Automations in the account. If there was a Cloud Connector before I am assuming it was a 'default' Cloud Connector administered by whoever takes care of Trial Accounts in general.

I have tried to contact Support but to do so it requires selecting a customer, and I do not have one - I am an SAP Employee and am using this account to prepare for the Certification. So I cannot contact Support through any standard channels

I cannot enable the Built In Support in BTP Cockpit due to below error.

 @SAPSupport any suggestions on how I can resolve this issue?

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Introduction to SAP DRC and Integration aspects
entry.content:
Bonjour à tous,

Je travaille actuellement avec SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (SAP DRC) et j'aimerais savoir s'il est possible d'adapter ou d'étendre la solution standard DRC pour répondre aux exigences légales et fiscales d'un pays qui n'est pas obligatoirement pris en charge par SAP.

Spécifiquement :

  • Existe-t-il des options disponibles pour créer un rapport ou un scénario personnalisé en RDC (par exemple, en créant une nouvelle activité de rapport ou un modèle de formulaire) ?

  • Existe-t-il des recommandations ou des bonnes pratiques pour développer une solution personnalisée au sein de SAP DRC tout en maintenant la conformité avec les normes SAP (en particulier pour la maintenance et les mises à niveau futures) ?

Merci beaucoup d'avance pour vos éclairages et partages d'expériences !

entry.title: Re: Accessing and Updating Transportation Charges SAP Transportation Management
entry.content:
@rajiv_kanoria : When can we expect SAP to provide functionality (API) to update the charges instead of using above logic?

There is a transportation charge node in TransportationOrderGenericRequest but as per the excel, that do not appear to be available for on-prem S/4 version. It fails with the error message

Updating transportation charges from external system not allowed (/SCMTMS/ACP Msg no 50)

We have used the above approach from the blog, but it does not work consistently. we get a message everything is successful, but data is not correctly updated.

entry.title: Re: SAP Inside Track Salvador 2025
entry.content: 👏

entry.title: Re: SAP Hana not starting after upgrade (localsecurestore...
entry.content:
Hi,

It looks like the localsecurestore service failure is what’s blocking your full HANA startup after the upgrade.

This usually happens because of one of these issues:

  • Corrupted secure store (SSFS) files.

  • Wrong file permissions on the secure store.

  • Disk space issues on the server.

Here’s what you can do

  1. Check the SSFS files under $(DIR_GLOBAL)/hdb/security/ssfs/.

    • Confirm that SSFS_HDB.DAT and SSFS_HDB.KEY are present.

    • Check that they are owned by the <sid>adm user and have correct permissions (typically 600).

  2. Check disk space:

    • Use df -h to make sure your /usr/sap/ and related file systems have free space.

  3. Check traces for more clues:

    • Look at daemon_trace, nameserver_trace, and indexserver_trace files.

  4. If files are corrupted:

    • You might need to restore the SSFS files from a backup if you have one.

    • If not, SAP Support will likely need to assist you to recreate or recover it safely.

Since you already raised an SAP case, keep it open. if it's a deeper SSFS corruption or version-specific issue with 2.00.083, they may also provide a patch or fix note

entry.title: Re: Extract configuration from Integration Design artifacts via API
entry.content: This sounds like a really useful project! Being able to visualize all the scheduled iFlows would definitely make it easier to manage and keep track of everything. Good luck with building it!

entry.title: Re: Flexible Workflows in SAP Purchase Requisition

another question is how we can made the PR content fix while it is under approval steps?

entry.title: Re: VAX/VAY and Cost element
entry.content: The VAX & VAY GL accounts have also been remained one of the areas of confusion for me as well but I found only solution avert this confusion which is to maintain the same GL account with cost element category in the both the modifiers ( VAX & VAY).

entry.title: Re: Updating Dimension Attributes in a model
entry.content:
Thanks @William_Yu for your response.

Background:
We use annual salaries from SF (loaded as Actuals) and split them into monthly forecasts using SAC Data Actions to calculate future YTD values.

New Requirement:

  • Use external YTD values for the last month of each quarter (e.g., March 2025).
  • Example: For April 2025, YTD = March’s YTD (from file) + April’s SAC-calculated salary.

Issue:

  • The Company Code is missing for the March 2025 YTD record (loaded from the file), causing reporting issues in the story.
  • Without Company Code, the story cannot display YTD values correctly.

Data Loaded from file

ID

Period

YTD

Data Src

Cost Center

Comp Code

1234

202503

45,000

PROLL_YTD

#

#

 After Copying to forecast

ID

Period

YTD

Data Src

Cost Center

Comp Code

1234

202503

45,000

WK-FCST

CC1

#

 Ask:
Need help dynamically updating the Company Code for these records (tried RESULTLOOKUP without success).

I hope I was able to better explain.

Thanks,

PS

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition
entry.content: Thank you very much

entry.title: Re: Depreciation expense decreasing the budget loaded in...
entry.content:
Hi,

If you do not want to record depreciation expenses on WBS element from assets, simply do not assign a WBS element to the asset but just a cost center. You also could define a validation rule to prevent erroneous input from end-users during asset creation.

Best regards,

 Mick

entry.title: Unveiling SAP Signavio's April 2025 Release: A Leap Forward in Business Process Transformation

entry.content: We have also got this requirement, didn't find any solution and we decided to create the Layout from scratch. Earlier the form was designed in Landscape mode so we decided to design new form from scratch in Portrait mode.

entry.title: Re: DUMP in SE37
entry.content: Same problem... any solution?

entry.title: Dynamic Intent Based Navigation
entry.content:
I created a List Report with a bunch of columns, one of which uses an Intent-based navigation to one of two external target pages.

 

Based on the value of the column, I need to navigate to a different target page, ie. a different semantic object-target.

Here's my cds annotation for one of the two targets:

@Consumption.semanticObject: 'ExpenseCenter'
@UI.lineItem: [{ position: 35, semanticObjectAction: 'manage', type: #WITH_INTENT_BASED_NAVIGATION }]   
@EndUserText.label: 'Source'
    SourceApp;

Let's say if the value is "Expense Center" I want it to link to ExpenseCenter-manage. If the value is "Checkin", it should point to Checkin-manage.

How can I make it point to a different semanticObject and Action based on a certain condition? The annotation @Consumption.semanticObject doesn't seem to accept an element, just a string literal.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Onboarding - Review New Hire Data - Assign Recruiter
entry.content:
Hi Adrian, have you tried using the dynamic group to define the responsible user for the data review and have the recruiter assigned, and then you can configure the business rule that routes the step to the created group- Assign a Responsible Group for Data Review (ResponsibleForNewHireDataReview).

Hope this helps.

entry.title: Re: 2505 C4C Release Content
entry.content:
Hi @pavla_bockova in the last webcast SAP said: "As of 2502, we are planning to transition the release quarterly communication to a blog Post release communication posted on the SAP Community instead of a quarterly webcast."

 

Regards, 

 

D.

entry.title: Re: Asset acquisition posting in special period 13 Public...
entry.content:
Hello @VanessaBrixle 

Thank you for sharing the screenshot!

Here’s a summary of the situation:

You are attempting to post an asset acquisition in period 13 of 2024 using the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud app “Manage Fixed Assets” (F0773), but you are encountering the error "Period 13 is not allowed."
You also noted that you cannot manually select or change the period in the app.

To clarify:

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the "Manage Fixed Assets" app only permits postings to normal periods, not to special periods (13–16).
Special periods are reserved strictly for year-end adjustments and can only be utilised via specific apps such as:

  • Post General Journal Entries (F0717)
  • Post with Clearing (F0892)
  • Manage Journal Entries (F0718)

Asset transactions (such as acquisitions, retirements, or transfers) cannot be posted directly to special periods through asset management apps.
This is a known system limitation.

Summary:

➔ It is impossible to post asset acquisitions to special periods (13–16) using the “Manage Fixed Assets” app.

Below are the options!

  • Post the acquisition in period 12 (the last normal period), or
  • (Alternative workaround) Post a manual journal entry in a special period for financial reporting purposes, although this will not correctly update the asset subledger (not ideal for asset management).

For more detailed information, you can refer to the SAP Help Portal:

Manage Fixed Assets - SAP Help Portal

Please let me know if you would like further information.

Best regards,
Chuma

entry.title: Re: Showing DIR Original file size zero
entry.content: Thank you Christopf. I have checked these recommendation. The first one is pointed to different AL11 path already for other use case and second one is set. However, my problem is, despite that it works in my lower sap landscape C1S and not only in Q1S. Could you suggest what could be the possibly wrong in Q1S. After debugging and comparing both landscapes, we found a FM called HTTP_POST_FILES_255 is showing permission denied and it does not get captured in SU53 or SLG1. Please suggest if there is a way to fix this.

entry.title: Re: BAPI Questions about creating new DIRs
entry.content: I have the same issue, works fine for new DIR version creation when executed by se37 but gives error when made RFC call. Returns error as "Document type XXX can only be assigned numbers internally". Please suggest how did you solve.

entry.title: Re: Week-Based (5-4-4) Calendar Year in S/4 HANA New Asset Accounting
entry.content:
Hi Nayamath, 

In 5-4-4 Calendar, we normally use 13 periods but SAP allows entries up to 24 to cover scenarios like shortened fiscal year(s), Fiscal year shift or asset accounting requirements where periods from two fiscal year might overlap. Maintaining the weightings ensures that depreciation distributes correctly across all the periods. This is a Standard behavior. You can adjust depends on the requirement. 

Hopes this clarifies and thanks for raising this.

entry.title: Re: Delta in DataFlows in SAP Datasphere
entry.content: thanks for the clarification

entry.title: Re: Assign tax indicators to tax condition types in SAP S...
entry.content: Hola!! He votado y he visto el documento segun el request 324204 con la solución provista por SAP. Pero en Argentina, al menos sigue No disponible un ID que reemplaza la OVK1 en S/4HANA Public, para poder configurar la determinación de secuencia de los impuestos por Pais en la master data de clientes y materiales. Y en el ID106589 de crear clasificaciones fiscales para cada condition type solo trae uno solo el TAR1. No me trae predeliverado los demas impuestos como TAR3, TAR4, etc para Argentina. Como lo han resuelto esto? .

entry.title: Re: Display the number of installments in the supplier item
entry.content:
Hi Nali,

In standard SAP S/4HANA and Fiori, you can view installment breakdowns mainly through:

  • Manage Supplier Line Items app

  • Manage Journal Entries app

When payment terms are set up with installments, the system automatically generates multiple open items  each showing the amount and due date separately.

Example:
If you post a supplier invoice for $600 with payment terms split into 3 installments, you’ll see 3 separate line items like:

  • $200 — due 04/25/2025

  • $200 — due 05/25/2025

  • $200 — due 06/25/2025

You can view this in Manage Supplier Line Items by filtering for the supplier and expanding the open items.

Hope this helps.

entry.title: Re: Generative AI with SAP – Part 1: Introduction to SAP Business AI
entry.content: Throughout the entire process of using third-party AI services, all data and functionalities are handled within the third party's data centers. Apart from contractual agreements, SAP has no technical measures  ensure the security of this process or to prevent data privacy breaches. How can this be considered secure? Unless SAP builds its own data centers or implements other technical solutions to ensure that all data and AI-related service calls can be monitored and controlled in real time by SAP or the customer, SAP's so-called security is nothing but empty talk

entry.title: Re: Overcoming the Lack of Long-Term Memory in Large Language Models (LLMs)
entry.content: Since SAP does not store conversation records within third-party large language models when utilizing them, how does SAP overcome the challenge of not being able to access previous contextual information or other relevant business data during each interaction with the large language model when using Joule? Without such context, how can the AI provide solutions that best serve the company’s long-term or overall interests?

entry.title: Re: Adobe Form/Smartform with RAP-Based OData Service Without SEGW

Dear Experts, There is one field called 'Item Category' in Service Order in Fiori. What is the main function for it? For example, if I selected 'Sales Item', what will be happen? 

Regards

Vincent

entry.title: Automate CPI Log Reporting: Extract Custom Header Properties and Load into SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
How to Extract CPI Message Logs with Custom Header Properties and Report in SAP Analytics Cloud

1. Problem Statement: Why You Can’t Rely on CPI Monitoring Alone

SAP’s Integration Suite (CPI) gives you visibility — but only just. If you want to check whether a message was delivered or inspect its Custom Header Properties, you can. But it’s manual. One message at a time. No consolidated view. No report.

Cloud ALM improves things slightly by centralising errors and alerts, but when it comes to reporting on message metadata like Custom Header Properties, you're stuck. Neither CPI’s monitor nor Cloud ALM’s Integration & Exception Monitor gives you a way to extract all this data in bulk.

And that’s a problem. Because sometimes your business needs answers like:

  • “Did all outbound invoice messages include the expected reference IDs?”
  • “Which messages failed for a specific customer or process?”
  • “Can we see everything we sent for this business object last month in one view?”

For many organisations, this isn’t just a nice-to-have — it’s a compliance or contractual requirement.

This is where the gap becomes critical: the data is there, but not accessible in a way that supports operational oversight, reporting, or auditing.

So, what’s the fix?

You build your own reporting pipeline — extracting message data via CPI’s APIs, enriching it with Custom Header Properties, and loading it into SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC).

2. Extracting Messages and Header Properties

To get the data you need out of SAP Cloud Integration (CPI), you’ll be working with two key APIs:

  • /MessageProcessingLogs to retrieve a list of processed messages. It is always advisable to test your APIs before using them in your application in Postman or Bruno for example:

  • /MessageProcessingLogs('{MessageGuid}')/CustomHeaderProperties to fetch Custom Header Properties for each message.

Step 1: Authenticate Against the CPI API

With basic authentication method, you can quickly test the API, however it's recommended to use a more secured method like OAuth 2.0 the way it's demonstrated in the attached script.

import requests
from requests.auth import HTTPBasicAuth

cpi_host = 'https://your-cpi-host.it-cpi.eu10.hana.ondemand.com'
username = 'your-technical-user'
password = 'your-password'

Step 2: Get a List of Message GUIDs

response = requests.get(
    f"{cpi_host}/api/v1/MessageProcessingLogs?$format=json",
    auth=HTTPBasicAuth(username, password)
)

messages = response.json().get('d', {}).get('results', [])

Step 3: Loop Through Messages to Fetch Header Properties

all_data = []

for msg in messages:
    guid = msg.get('MessageGuid')
    hdr_response = requests.get(
        f"{cpi_host}/api/v1/MessageProcessingLogs('{guid}')/CustomHeaderProperties?$format=json",
        auth=HTTPBasicAuth(username, password)
    )
    
    custom_headers = hdr_response.json().get('d', {}).get('results', [])
    
    record = {
        'MessageGuid': guid,
        'IntegrationFlowName': msg.get('IntegrationFlowName'),
        'Timestamp': msg.get('LogStart'),
        'CustomHeaderProperties': {hdr['Name']: hdr['Value'] for hdr in custom_headers}
    }
    
    all_data.append(record)

Step 4: Store the Data

import pandas as pd

df = pd.json_normalize(all_data, sep='_')
df.to_csv('cpi_message_headers.csv', index=False)

3. Preparing Your SAC Model

To import the data you extracted out of SAP Cloud Integration (CPI) in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC), you’ll be working with the following APIs:

  • GET all Models with api/v1/dataimport/models endpoint to return a list of all Models
  • GET Model Details with api/v1/dataimport/models/{modelID} endpoint to give us the types of data we can import into this model
  • GET Model Metadata with api/v1/dataimport/models/{modelID}/metadata endpoint
  • POST - Create a Job to be used later to import the data with api/v1/dataimport/models/{modelID}/importType endpoint
  • POST - Load data into a job with api/v1/dataimport/jobs/{jobID} endpoint
  • GET the list of jobs with api/v1/dataimport/jobs endpoint
  • POST - Validate with api/v1/dataimport/jobs/{jobID}/validate endpoint
  • POST - Run the job with api/v1/dataimport/jobs/{jobID}/run endpoint
  • GET the status of the run with api/v1/dataimport/jobs/{jobID}/status endpoint

However, there is a simple and quick way to import the data in one click mode with the api/v1/import/{modelID} endpoint. This method is demonstrated in the sample attached script.

Before you can load data into SAC, you must ensure the model exists and that you’ve verified its metadata.

Step 1: Get Model Info

model_id = 'your-model-id'
sac_host = 'https://<your-sac-tenant>.sapbusinessobjects.cloud'
token = 'your-bearer-token'

headers = {
    'Authorization': f'Bearer {token}'
}

response = requests.get(
    f'{sac_host}/api/v1/dataimport/models/{model_id}',
    headers=headers
)

print(response.json())

Step 2: Get Model Metadata (Field Types)

meta_response = requests.get(
    f'{sac_host}/api/v1/dataimport/models/{model_id}/metadata',
    headers=headers
)

metadata = meta_response.json()

4. Setting Up the Data Import Job

Step 1: Create the Import Job

import json

job_payload = {
    "importType": "Append"  # or "Full"
}

create_job_response = requests.post(
    f'{sac_host}/api/v1/dataimport/models/{model_id}/importType',
    headers={**headers, 'Content-Type': 'application/json'},
    data=json.dumps(job_payload)
)

job_info = create_job_response.json()
job_id = job_info.get('id')

print(f"Created import job with ID: {job_id}")

5. Loading Data into the Job

Step 1: Prepare the Payload

data_payload = {
    "data": [
        {
            "MessageGuid": "12345",
            "IntegrationFlowName": "InvoiceDispatch",
            "Timestamp": "2024-12-01T10:15:00Z",
            "BusinessObjectID": "INV-1001"
        },
        {
            "MessageGuid": "12346",
            "IntegrationFlowName": "InvoiceDispatch",
            "Timestamp": "2024-12-01T10:16:00Z",
            "BusinessObjectID": "INV-1002"
        }
    ]
}

Step 2: Load the Data Into the Job

load_response = requests.post(
    f'{sac_host}/api/v1/dataimport/jobs/{job_id}',
    headers={**headers, 'Content-Type': 'application/json'},
    data=json.dumps(data_payload)
)

if load_response.status_code == 202:
    print("Data loaded into job successfully.")
else:
    print(f"Error loading data: {load_response.status_code} - {load_response.text}")

6. Managing and Monitoring Jobs

Step 1: Validate the Job

validate_response = requests.post(
    f'{sac_host}/api/v1/dataimport/jobs/{job_id}/validate',
    headers=headers
)

if validate_response.status_code == 202:
    print("Validation successful. Ready to run the job.")
else:
    print(f"Validation failed: {validate_response.status_code} - {validate_response.text}")

Step 2: Run the Job

run_response = requests.post(
    f'{sac_host}/api/v1/dataimport/jobs/{job_id}/run',
    headers=headers
)

if run_response.status_code == 202:
    print("Job started successfully.")
else:
    print(f"Failed to start the job: {run_response.status_code} - {run_response.text}")

7. Checking Job Status

Step 1: Poll the Job Status

import time

while True:
    status_response = requests.get(
        f'{sac_host}/api/v1/dataimport/jobs/{job_id}/status',
        headers=headers
    )

    status_info = status_response.json()
    status = status_info.get('status')

    print(f"Current job status: {status}")

    if status in ["Completed", "Failed"]:
        break

    time.sleep(5)

Conclusion

If you’ve ever felt stuck manually digging through CPI logs just to answer simple business questions, now you know there’s a better way.

By using SAP’s APIs, a little Python, and the SAC Data Import API, you can build a repeatable pipeline to extract, load, and report on Custom Header Properties — at scale.

The steps you followed — extracting with /MessageProcessingLogs, staging data for SAC, validating, running, and monitoring — create a blueprint you can extend for other use cases too.

The data was always there. Now, you control it.

A sample Python script is attached to this blog, however, the script is following the one-click approach to import the data into SAC.

Common Mistakes and How to Avoid Them

  • Mismatched Field Names: Match your payload keys exactly to the SAC model field names (case-sensitive).
  • Wrong Date Format: Use ISO 8601 date format (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ).
  • Not Validating Before Running: Always validate your job to catch issues early.
  • Overwriting When You Meant to Append: Use Append unless you intend to replace all data.
  • Token Expiry: Ensure your OAuth token is valid or refresh it if needed.

entry.title: 寻找SAP中VLPOD对应的BAPI
entry.content: 我想寻找一个BAPI或者函数，可以执行SAP 事务码VLPOD。并且可以生成后续凭证，因为我们系统的交货证明是采用的两步法过账。我目前使用的是WS_DELIVERY_UPDATE_2  ，但是它只能成功更新数量和状态，并不会生成601的物料凭证及会计凭证

entry.title: Re: Updating Dimension Attributes in a model
entry.content: Sorry, still not fully understand your problem here. But I assume anyway you need maintain the relation between the empolyee ID and company code in master data like you did for cost center.

Hi @jgleichmann 

I agree. Thanks again for your comment. I've added a "Definitions" section to the content. Naming conventions are also important (standard [offline] partitioning and [online] partitioning), so I tried to mention that as well.

Regards,
Laszlo

entry.title: Error "Unable to recognize the artifact type [notificationtemplate]" when creating a form in Process
entry.content:
I encountered the following error while working in SAP Build Process Automation:

"Request failed with status code 400. Unable to recognize the artifact type [notification template]."

Previously, I could create forms and use them normally. However, now when I create a form, it only appears in the Overview section and does not show up in the Process section, so I cannot add it into my process.

Thank you very much for your support!

ASAP
ASAP
ASAP
BW_MAST
BDS_DB10
CRMORDER
CRM_WEBREQ
SMD
SMD
SOLAR01

entry.title: Re: Extract configuration from Integration Design artifacts via API
entry.content:
Hello Mateusz,

I modifed this function:

def getTenantInfo(url){
    def map = [:]
    tenantValues = url =~ /(?<=https:\/\/)(.+?(?=-))(.+?(?<=\.hci[a-z][a-z][a-z].)(.+?(?=\.hana)))/
    map.id = tenantValues[0..-1][0][1]
    map.region = tenantValues[0..-1][0][3]
    map.tmnURL = "https://${map.id}-tmn.hci.${map.region}.hana.ondemand.com"
    return map
}

Which creates the tmn (management) URL for Neo environment.

At the moment I didn't make it dynamically and just hardcode the one of my tenant.

    map.id = "xxx"
    map.region = "yyy"
    map.tmnURL = "https://xxx.it-cpi001.cfapps.yyy.hana.ondemand.com"

id and region are used in other places so I have defined separately.

If you don't know where to find yours you can refer to this link:

https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-digital-manufacturing/integration-guide/how-to-get-sap-cloud-integration-management-url

Last but not least, you will need to solve the warnings of the groovy editor, otherwise you cannot apply the previous changes. For that you can remove the unused libraries and adapt the one using wildcards:

Before:

import groovy.json.*

I added only this one, but I have to check if more are needed:

import groovy.json.JsonOutput

It is important to mention that in CF you cannot paste the iFlow runtime url in the browser and that's it, but in CF you will need to authenticate first with your clientID and secret from your service key.

Hope it helps

Let me know if you manage to display the schedule table.

Best regards

Javier

entry.title: Issue Displaying Lumira Dashboards After Upgrade to BO 4.3 SP04 Patch 11
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

After upgrading to BO 4.3 SP04 Patch 11, we're facing an issue where Lumira dashboards don't display for non-admin users (admin users are fine). SAP Note 3268641 was applied but didn’t resolve it.
Attached a screenshot of the error.
Has anyone else faced a similar problem after this upgrade? Are there any additional steps, authorizations, or configurations needed beyond what is mentioned in the SAP Note?

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: unable to create connection from SF to SAC & datasphere
entry.content:

I have searched many articular, but none has been very useful. i have screenshot from SF side with Oauth2 setup and SAC. the error i get is : Unable to connect to datasource. Check the connection details and user credentials, and then try again.

the question does this connection looks right and the USER id does it need special step that i have missed?

entry.title: Issue in SAP Build Process Automation action sending empty list payload to SAP CAP action.
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

We are building a SAP CAP application where we define an action to store list data into a HANA DB after an approval step in SAP Build Process Automation (BPA).

Here is the setup.
My CAP application schema looks like this.
entity OTPDA : managed {
key userID : String;
issueDate : String;
payComponent : String;
payComponentNature : String;
amount : String;
currency : String;
unit : String;
tmu : String;
assignmentNumber : String;
comments : String;
log : String;
}


CAP action in service section
action storeOtpdaData(OtpdaDatas : array of OTPDA) returns String;

and its handler

const onStoreOtpdaData = async (req) => {
const { OTPDA } = cds.entities("db.payroll");
const tx = cds.transaction(req);
const aOtpdaDatas = req.data.OtpdaDatas;

try {
for (const entry of aOtpdaDatas) {
await tx.run(
UPSERT.into(OTPDA).entries({
userID: entry.userID,
issueDate: entry.issueDate,
payComponent: entry.payComponent,
payComponentNature: entry.payComponentNature,
amount: entry.amount,
currency: entry.currency,
unit: entry.unit,
tmu: entry.tmu,
assignmentNumber: entry.assignmentNumber,
comments: entry.comments,
log: entry.log
})
);
}
return `${aOtpdaDatas.length} record(s) stored successfully.`;
} catch (error) {
console.error("Error inserting OTPDA data:", error);
req.reject(500, "Failed to store OTPDA records.");
}
};

In SAP Build Process Automation, I have created an action using the CAP destination and mapped it accordingly. However, when BPA tries to call the CAP action, the request payload is going blank, and therefore no records are being stored.

Here is the screenshot:- The request section payload structure is array of object  but the request body which BPA is sending is array of blank objects. :-


Any insights or guidance would be highly appreciated.
Thanks a lot in advance!

SAP Build Process Automation SAP Cloud Application Programming Model SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA database

And the app "Supplier invoice list" after click "edit" have the same question.

entry.title: 🚀 SAP Green Token Onboarding Page Now Live!
entry.content:
We’re thrilled to announce that the Onboarding page for SAP Green Token is now live on the SAP Support Portal!

If you're supporting a customer, partner, or working with Green Token yourself — this is your new starting point for a smooth and successful onboarding journey.

 

✅What’s inside?

The Green Token Onboarding Resource Center brings together all essential self-service assets in one central hub. It’s designed to guide users through every stage of the onboarding process:

  • Technical Setup – Understand prerequisites, system access, and user roles

  • Enablement Resources – Access demos, tutorials, and solution overview materials

  • Implementation Guidance – Step-by-step help to launch your first use case

  • Go-Live Support – Best practices to ensure adoption and long-term success

Whether you’re a customer, partner, or internal team member involved in sustainability or supply chain initiatives — this resource makes it easier to get started and scale faster with Green Token.

 

📎Explore the onboarding page here:
👉https://support.sap.com/en/product/onboarding-resource-center/green-token.html

 

💬Questions or feedback?

Comment on this blog — we’d love to hear your thoughts, experiences, or suggestions to keep improving the onboarding experience!

Let’s make sustainability traceable, transparent, and easier to adopt — together. 🌍

 

Hi! 👋 I’m Gloria Figaroa. Since 2015, I live and breathe sustainability and supply chain management. 🍃 I’ve had the pleasure to work on Green Token for the past 4+ years as a Product Manager. Now in my latest role as Product Marketing Manager, I take care of everything Go-to-Market. 🌍 Follow me to stay up to date on the SAP Green Token solution, its newest features and functions, and detailed overviews of its capabilities. You will hear it first here! 📢

entry.title: Re: How to turn off the 'Add Comments | Refactor' automat...
entry.content:
Hi Mihaly,

Today it is not possible to turn off  'Add Comments | Refactor' actions. We will add setting to enable turning it off and release it in next BAS release. I'll update you once it will be available in production landscapes.

Regards,

Rima

With each such scientific work, I wonder how close we are to a vision that just over a year ago seemed like complete science fiction:


BR

Kind regards 

Simon

entry.title: How to Calculate the Total Value of Selected Pay Components in SAP SuccessFactors Business Rules
entry.content:
Requirement
I need to calculate the combined value of 10 specific pay components in SAP SuccessFactors Compensation. Right now, my rule breaks whenever one of those components is missing or null. The only way I’ve found to work around it is to write IF blocks for every possible combination (for example:

  • “If Basic Pay and Housing are not null, then Total Salary = Basic Pay + Housing”
  • “If Basic Pay and Bonus are not null, then Total Salary = Basic Pay + Bonus”
  • …and so on for dozens of permutations),
    which is unmanageable.

What I Want
A simple Business Rule pattern that:

  1. Defines my 10 pay component fields once,
  2. Automatically treats any missing/null component as zero,
  3. Sums only the non-null components,
  4. Never fails when a component is absent,
  5. Doesn’t require writing dozens of IF-combinations or using pay-component groups.

Example of Desired Behavior

  • Employee A has Basic Pay = 5,000, Housing Allowance = 2,000, all other components null → Total = 7,000
  • Employee B has Bonus = 1,500, Travel Allowance = 800, all others null → Total = 2,300
  • Employee C has none of the 10 components → Total = 0

How can I implement this “sum only non-null fields” logic in a single, maintainable Business Rule? What best practices or built-in functions can I leverage so I don’t have to hard-code every combination manually?

Attached sample Business rule

entry.title: Re: SAC Story breaks when Datasphere model is updated
entry.content: Hi, thanks for the answer, we were facing the same issue, and indeed restoring to a prev. version did solve the issue. is there a wat to see the "new internal ID" in the DataSphere or maybe see an alert if this kind of behavior occurs ? for example updating on a change in this "new internal ID"?

entry.title: Re: New Here

entry.title: Re: Starter / Noob
entry.content: @ArunPrasathR Welcome:). Which of the topics listed here are most relevant for you: https://pages.community.sap.com/topics ?

entry.title: Building Ethical AI in SAP: Tackling Bias and Ensuring Compliance
entry.content:
The Ethical Imperative for AI in SAP

Ethical AI refers to the practice of designing and deploying artificial intelligence systems in ways that align with values such as fairness, accountability, and transparency. Within SAP environments, where AI may influence everything from employee evaluations to procurement decisions, ensuring ethical alignment is more than a technical concern—it's a business necessity.

Failure to account for ethics can lead to:

  • Discriminatory outcomes in hiring and promotions.
  • Breaches of data privacy.
  • Regulatory penalties under GDPR or other privacy laws.
  • Erosion of stakeholder trust and brand damage.

1. Tackling Algorithmic Bias

The Problem

AI models learn from historical data—data that may be riddled with past human biases. In SAP SuccessFactors, for example, machine learning algorithms used in talent acquisition could perpetuate gender or racial bias if trained on skewed datasets.

Solutions

  • Bias Audits: Implement regular audits of AI outputs to identify patterns of unfair treatment across protected characteristics.

  • Diverse Training Data: Ensure training datasets represent the diversity of your workforce or customer base.

  • Explainable AI (XAI): Use techniques that make AI decisions interpretable, allowing human reviewers to understand and challenge automated outcomes.

SAP’s AI Ethics Advisory Panel and its “Guiding Principles for Artificial Intelligence” emphasize fairness and inclusivity, but businesses must go further to operationalize these values within their specific configurations.

2. Ensuring Data Privacy and GDPR Compliance

The Problem

SAP systems process vast volumes of sensitive data—customer preferences, employee records, financial transactions. When AI models ingest this data, risks emerge around unauthorized access, misuse, or non-compliance with privacy regulations like the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).

Solutions

  • Data Minimization: Only collect and use data strictly necessary for the task at hand.

  • Anonymization and Encryption: Use encryption techniques and anonymized datasets during training to reduce exposure.

  • Consent Management: Align AI use cases with SAP’s consent frameworks and tools such as SAP Customer Data Cloud to ensure users’ rights are protected.

SAP supports GDPR compliance through tools like the SAP Data Privacy Governance solution and provides built-in capabilities for data masking, logging, and access controls—but these need to be configured and regularly audited.

3. Building Governance Frameworks

The Problem

Without clear governance structures, ethical lapses can go unnoticed until reputational or financial damage occurs. Many enterprises lack a dedicated framework to oversee AI development and deployment within SAP.

Solutions

  • AI Ethics Committees: Form cross-functional teams including data scientists, legal, HR, and compliance professionals to review AI initiatives.

  • AI Lifecycle Management: Use SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) to implement continuous monitoring and version control over AI models.

  • Ethics by Design: Embed ethical checkpoints throughout the AI development lifecycle—from data sourcing to deployment and monitoring.

A mature governance framework ensures that ethical considerations are baked into AI workflows—not bolted on after the fact.

4. Transparency and Accountability

The Problem

Many AI systems operate as "black boxes," making decisions that are difficult for users to understand or challenge. This opacity undermines trust and complicates compliance with regulations that require to be explainable.

Solutions

  • Model Documentation: Maintain clear records of how models are trained, which variables are used, and what assumptions were made.

  • User Feedback Loops: Enable employees or customers interacting with AI in SAP systems to flag suspicious outputs or suggest corrections.

  • Audit Trails: Leverage SAP's logging and audit functionalities to track decision paths and support investigations when needed.

SAP’s AI Core and AI Launchpad offer foundational capabilities for tracking and documenting model behavior, but organizations must actively integrate these tools into their compliance strategies.

A Call to Responsible Innovation

As enterprises accelerate AI adoption within SAP environments, the challenge is clear: power must be tempered with principles. Ethical AI isn’t just about compliance—it’s about earning trust, improving decision-making, and building systems that reflect the values of your business and society at large.

By proactively tackling bias, safeguarding privacy, embedding governance, and ensuring transparency, organizations can unlock the true potential of AI in SAP—securely and ethically.

Recommended Tools & Resources

  • SAP AI Ethics Guidelines

  • SAP Data Privacy Governance

  • SAP AI Core and AI Launchpad

entry.title: How to fill the fields KATYP, MGEFL and MSEHI in the segment E1SKB1M of GLMAST01?
entry.content:
I'm creating a custom program which creates G/L accounts (FS00, tables SKA1, etc.) by creating an Inbound IDoc of type GLMAST01, and to make it easier, I'm using a G/L account created manually, by looking at the columns of the tables SKA1, SKAT and SKB1 and passing their values to the eponymous fields of the segments E1SKA1M, E1SKATM and E1SKB1M.

NB: it has been decided to create a custom program, instead of using migration tools like LSMW, Migration Cockpit or whatever, for some reasons I won't discuss here.

It's all fine, except that I don't understand how to fill the fields KATYP, MGEFL and MSEHI in the segment E1SKB1M, there are no corresponding columns in the table SKB1.

Thanks.

Sandra

System: S/4HANA ONPREMISE 2023 FPS 01.

NB: there is also the field RESERVE of 20 characters (in the segments E1SKA1M and E1SKB1M), which seems to not be used now but I guess it's reserved for feature use, as can be deduced for instance from the description in the note 1515852 - ALE FIDCCP02: Tax reporting date is not sent - SAP for Me (E1FIKPF-RESERVE in IDOC message types FIDCC1 and FIDCC2.

entry.title: Re: How to fill the fields KATYP, MGEFL and MSEHI in the...
entry.content:
The 3 fields KATYP, MGEFL and MSEHI of the segment E1SKB1M come from the table CSKB.

Fill them the same way as done by the function module MASTERIDOC_CREATE_GLMAST (it's the one called by the transaction BD18 which generates outbound IDocs for existing G/L accounts):

 

        IF lv_glaccount_type <> if_gl_account_master=>gc_glaccount_type-pl_neutral.
          SELECT SINGLE katyp mgefl msehi INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF e1skb1m
          FROM cskb JOIN tka02 ON tka02~kokrs = cskb~kokrs "#EC CI_BUFFJOIN
          WHERE tka02~bukrs = skb1key-bukrs
          AND   cskb~kstar  = skb1key-saknr
          AND   cskb~datbi >= sy-datum
          AND   cskb~datab <= sy-datum.
        ENDIF.
        IF sy-subrc <> 0 OR
           lv_glaccount_type = if_gl_account_master=>gc_glaccount_type-pl_neutral.
          e1skb1m-katyp = '!'.
        ELSEIF e1skb1m-msehi IS NOT INITIAL.
          CALL FUNCTION 'UNIT_OF_MEASURE_SAP_TO_ISO'
            EXPORTING
              sap_code = e1skb1m-msehi
            IMPORTING
              iso_code = e1skb1m-msehi
            EXCEPTIONS
              OTHERS   = 0.
        ENDIF.

 

LV_GLACCOUNT_TYPE is SKA1-GLACCOUNT_TYPE and the constant PL_NEUTRAL is "N".

The table TKA02 assigns one controlling area for each company code, which is needed to join SKB1 (key: G/L account and company code) to CSKB (key: cost element and controlling area).

entry.title: Re: FSCM CM: How transaction date in SD objects
entry.content: Yes, this note refers to the transaction date (fqm_flow-transaction_date) calculation for sales orders. And yes, it considers the Payment history as well. Please see if you can use the BAdi BADI_FQM_FLOW_ADJUST_CORE to customize the date in table FQM_FLOW.

entry.title: Fixed Asset : Ledger Settings
entry.content:
When setting up the Assets in Development we configured the 3 Ledgers 0L, 2L and 4G 

After all the Transports to Test System only 0L and 2L is available for Assets . How do we fix this please ?

entry.title: Re: What is the main function for 'Item Cateogry' in the...
entry.content:
Hi,

 

for such queries I would always first have a look on the help portal and read the official fiori app documentation.

It states:

"An item category specifies the properties and attributes of each item. You use item categories to determine how items are processed, for example, the manner in which the items are billed."

 

Source: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/c9b5e9de6e674fb99fff88d72c352291/f6ba8a0022f0443bb02e176857bf4d5d.html

 

Best,

Edrilan Berisha

entry.title: Re: Questionaire for Change log of Profit Segment in Sale...

this is not the SAP Support, this is the SAP Community.

Anyway, what exactly is now your problem? With this description, it's hard to give you a solution or provide help.

You don't want that any user is changes the sales office of the line item? Or what is your problem?

Best,

Edrilan Berisha

similar question have been raised already: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/condition-type-0263-no-update-type-for-outgoing-payment-error/qaq-p/9080514

entry.title: How to send an event with SAP NetWeaver Add-On for Event enablement 1.0?
entry.content:
I followed the configuration steps of the SAP NetWeaver Add-On for Event enablement 1.0, as described here:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP%20NetWeaver%20Add-On%20for%20Event%20enablement%201.0/e966e6c0e61443ebaa0270a4bae4b363/53d14e7af03f436a8dbf6072928ed4bf.html

I tried to send a notification event for Sales Order Change Event, as described here:
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/sap-enterprise-messaging-for-sap-erp-howto-guide-part-2-first-use-case/ba-p/13463694

Unfortunately, it does not work, even though I have done all the steps correctly (I suppose). Checking transactions /ASADEV/ACI_MONITOR or to SLG1 does not show me anything (also no errors). My questions is: What can I do now, to identify the problem? I checked all steps steps, in case I missed something and now I am unsure how my further error analysis shall look like. Would be great if someone can provide some help.

Hello, 

Have you activated the CDS view from below documentation:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/3920dc4873e04383895bf2459163ee26.html?locale=en-US

entry.title: Re: This file has limited permissions. Error in Fiori
entry.content:
Hello Dear, 

This is as per SAP standard design as explained in below SAP Note, 

if you want to fix this issue follow SAP Notes, 

https://me.sap.com/notes/2724522

https://me.sap.com/notes/2395509

https://me.sap.com/notes/2464364

Thank you, 

Sreenivas

entry.title: Re: Using SAP approuter with server-sent events
entry.content: Hi, that's a great finding.Which kind of your approuter is, a standardlone or managed one? I got the same problem when I deployed my app to SAP Workzone, And I set the compression property in the xs-app.json on app level not works.However if I use a standardlone approuter it works.

entry.title: Install on-premise partner add-ons for S/4HANA 2023 private cloud
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

We had released add-ons(partner) for our customers and they are on-premise. 

Is there any difference in installing the partner add-ons on S/4HANA private cloud or its same as like for on-premise? 

What is the procedure to release private cloud compatible add-ons and customers to install them on private cloud?

Thanks,

Prabhakaran

entry.title: Migration EC-CS to GR and Mapping
entry.content:
Hi Experts,
We are currently planning an EC-CS to Group Reporting migration, working closely with SAP Support and using the migration note 2833748.
Asking if the hierarchy maintained in CX17 Display Cons COA can continue to support or we will use the GR apps exclusively.
Currently use FC10 Financial statements with the CX17 created hierarchy to view both GL and FSItem structured financial statements.
In a regular GR implementation we use the Map FSI with GL accounts, Define FS items and manage global hierarchies to manage the mapping/ FS Item management/ hierarchy management steps.
Thanks
Eyal

entry.title: Re: OSGI Events in SAP Integration Suite
entry.content: Well done. Really interesting stuff.

entry.title: Errores al enviar el fichero de bases a la seguridad social española
entry.content:
Hola a todos:
Este mes hemos tenido errores al enviar el fichero de bases a la seguridad social española, y es porque tal como tenemos configurado el cálculo de las bases de cotización para el personal que tiene IT, accidente, maternidad, etc. calcula la base de CCCC y Base CCPP como la base reguladora x número de días del mes, y nos informa la seguridad social que debemos calcularla como Base reguladora x 30 días siempre, tenga 30, 31 o 28 días el mes. ¿Cómo podemos configurar esto? Pongo un ejemplo del mes de Febrero de una trabajadora que está enferma desde abril de 2024 y cuya base reguladora diaria es 60,43€, y SAP nos calcula 60,43 x 28 días = 1.692,04€ debería calcular 60,43 x 30 días = 1.812,90€.

Hemos visto que en la tabla V_T5EU3 podemos configurar SDADJ Criterio de ajuste de días, y lo teníamos con la opción 01 Ajuste según criterio legal, y lo hemos cambiado a 02 Ajuste según SLD p/ todos CCC, pero no hemos notado ningún cambio.

Agradeceré cualquier ayuda al respecto. Gracias y saludos.

Javier

entry.title: Re: Fixed Asset : Ledger Settings
entry.content:
Hello @GerhardK ,

Thank you for sharing the error message.  You mentioned that you had configured the 3 Ledgers 0L, 2L and 4G, but 4G might not be fully activated! Please follow the instructions below

The main Cause:

  • In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, additional ledgers (like 4G) must be configured before legacy data transfer and must be actively available in the Asset Accounting (FI-AA) settings across all systems.
  • Transports in S/4HANA Cloud may not automatically carry all ledger-dependent settings, particularly for Asset Accounting.
  • The Ledger Group / Ledger Settings under Asset Accounting (IMG > Financial Accounting > Asset Accounting > Organisational Settings) need to be consistent across Dev, Test, and Production.
  • Likely, 4G was not activated or assigned correctly to the company code/chart of depreciation level in the Test system.

Solution Steps:

You need to manually activate 4G Ledger for Asset Accounting in the Test system.

Here’s exactly what to do:

Check Ledger 4G Configuration:

  • Go to SSCUI / App: “Manage Your Solution”.
  • Navigate to Finance > Asset Accounting > Organizational Settings.
  • Check if Ledger 4G is assigned to your Company Code and Chart of Depreciation.

If Ledger 4G is missing:

  • Open SSCUI / App: “Specify Ledger for Accounting Principle”.
  • Check if Accounting Principle for Ledger 4G is correctly mapped.
  • If not, create/match the mapping manually in Test system.

Activate Ledger Group Settings:

  • In Asset Accounting Settings, activate the ledger 4G under “Ledger Settings for Asset Accounting.”
  • Ensure that 4G has the Fiscal Year Variant and Planning Ledger filled out.

Transport again or Manual Fix:

  • In S/4HANA Cloud Public, a manual adjustment is sometimes required because some objects are not automatically transported (due to restrictions in Cloud Control Center).
  • After manual adjustment, use “Consistent Check” to validate Asset Accounting settings.
  1. Perform Data Reconciliation:
  • After fixing, run reconciliation reports between General Ledger and Asset Accounting (e.g., FAGLGVTR) to ensure everything matches.

Please Note:

If you have already started a Legacy Data Transfer in the Test system, reset the transfer and redo it after the ledger fix.

Otherwise, the system will not allow changing the ledger setup after initial asset postings.

Official SAP Help reference for your case:

SAP Help: Additional Ledgers in Asset Accounting (S/4HANA Cloud)

You need to activate Ledger 4G manually in the Asset Accounting settings of the Test system under “Organisational Settings” and check the ledger-accounting principle mapping.

If Legacy Transfer has already started, reset it first.

Let me know if it works

Thank you, and kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: Error "Unable to recognize the artifact type [notificationtemplate]" when creating a f
entry.content: I am also facing the same issue. Any solutions/?

entry.title: Reimagining the Future of Learning: Strategic Insights from the SAP SuccessFactors Learning 1H 2025
entry.content:
As SAP SuccessFactors rolls out its 1H 2025 Learning updates, one thing is clear: we're not just adding new features — we’re laying the foundation for the future of workforce development.
AI, skills intelligence, and modernised learning experiences are no longer "nice to have" — they’re essentials for organisations aiming to stay competitive.

While it’s easy to get caught up in the "what’s new," I want to dig deeper into the why behind these changes — and what it means for learning leaders focused on future-proofing their organisations.

Reimagining Learning with Embedded AI

The new AI-Assisted Image Generation for content creation isn’t just a cool tool — it marks a real shift in how learning teams work.

Instead of just managing content, learning teams can now create smarter, faster, and more engaging learning experiences.

With AI woven into everyday tasks, teams can:

  • Cut down production time for high-quality content

  • Quickly update visuals at scale

  • Bring storytelling closer to skills growth journeys through Growth Portfolios

This is a real move towards hyper-personalised, dynamic learning ecosystems — and it challenges L&D leaders to rethink their approach to content creation and operations.

Skills-Based Learning: Making It Real

The new Proficiency-Based Learning Suggestions embed learning recommendations directly into Growth Portfolios, guided by real-time skills data.

This isn’t just a user interface upgrade — it’s about turning skills-first strategies into daily practice.

For organisations focused on building a skills-driven culture, this removes a major roadblock: connecting what employees need to learn with why it matters to their career growth.

To fully tap into this, organisations will need:

  • A cleaner, well-managed skills taxonomy

  • Smarter, more targeted learning content

  • Stronger collaboration between learning, talent, and skills teams

Conversational AI: A New Edge for Learning

Bringing Joule into learning workflows — like course approvals and mandatory training access — changes the game.

In today’s mobile-first, attention-scarce world, enabling learners and managers to get things done through simple conversations is a major advantage.

 

Organisations that adopt conversational learning early will stand out.
Now is the time for learning teams to pilot Joule use cases across onboarding, reskilling, and career development programmes.

Managing Deprecations: A Strategic Priority

Several older features — like Item Details and Learning News — will be retired over the next 12–18 months.

This isn't just technical cleanup. It’s a wake-up call.

Key questions leaders need to ask:

  • Are your learning home pages ready for full rollout?

  • Have you migrated all your legacy assets to the new Course Home and Growth Portfolio?

  • Is your team ready to design experiences using the latest banners, search, and ILX frameworks?

Treating these changes as "just IT tasks" is risky. Strategic L&D leaders will use this moment to accelerate modernisation, improve learner engagement, and align more closely with business goals.

Strategic Focus Areas for 2025

Here’s where learning leaders should focus:

Focus Area Why It Matters What to Do
Embed AI into Content OperationsCreate faster, more learner-centric experiencesActivate AI image generation and update workflows
Shift from Courses to CapabilitiesSkills are the new career currencyLeverage Growth Portfolios and skills-gap aligned learning
Pilot Conversational LearningMeet learners where they areIntegrate Joule into the learner journey
Modernise the Learning ExperienceOld interfaces hurt adoptionTransition fully to the new Learning Home and Course Home
Treat Deprecations as StrategicIt's not optional — it's criticalRun a Learning Modernisation Audit before 2H 2025

Final Thoughts: Learning at the Speed of Business

The SAP SuccessFactors 1H 2025 Learning release isn’t just another update.

It’s a call to action: to rethink how learning fits into the employee experience — not as a standalone system, but as a powerful enabler of organisational agility.

The future belongs to those who can adapt learning to individual journeys, powered by intelligent systems and aligned to business needs.

The real question for learning leaders isn’t "Which features should we turn on?" It’s "What workforce outcomes are we driving — and how quickly can we get there?"

#StayAhead #LearningTransformation #SkillsFirst #SAPSuccessFactors #BusinessAI #HXM #FutureOfLearning

You can opt this as SAP ABAP development certification.

entry.title: Change data in the dropdown list or searchHelp with draft in RAP in Odata v4gets the old text again
entry.content:
In Edit mode of an instance with draft in RAP in Odata , I am trying to change the data on dropdown list or in the search help it gets back the old text ( this issue not happens in Odata v2 ) .

ex : its selected already as 'Forum' when i am trying to change it to 'Workshop'  the text gets back to 'Forum' again but if i saved the data it makes it correct as 'Workshop'  . 

 

Can any one help ?

entry.title: Re: RAP : Trying to make a child with composition relatio...
entry.content: @ShivamChadha if you can help in this blog : https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/change-data-in-the-dropdown-list-or-searchhelp-with-draft-in-rap-in-odata/qaq-p/14087575#M4911996
